conditions.name: Insulin Resistance
conditions.description: Cells fail to respond normally to insulin; glucose uptake impaired
conditions.functional_root: Visceral fat, chronic inflammation, mitochondrial dysfunction, sleep deprivation, excess refined carbohydrate
conditions.conventional_approach: Lifestyle modification, metformin if progressed to T2D

conditions.name: Type 2 Diabetes
conditions.description: Chronic hyperglycaemia due to insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency
conditions.functional_root: Advanced insulin resistance; often reversible in early stages through lifestyle
conditions.conventional_approach: Metformin first line, GLP-1 agonists, insulin

conditions.name: Metabolic Syndrome
conditions.description: Cluster: central obesity, hypertension, high triglycerides, low HDL, insulin resistance
conditions.functional_root: Chronic inflammation + HPA dysregulation + poor metabolic flexibility
conditions.conventional_approach: Address each component separately; lifestyle is primary

conditions.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions.description: Underactive thyroid; insufficient T3/T4 production
conditions.functional_root: Often autoimmune (Hashimoto's); iodine or selenium deficiency; chronic stress suppressing T4→T3 conversion
conditions.conventional_approach: Levothyroxine (T4). Many patients feel better on T3+T4 combination — often not offered conventionally

conditions.name: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis
conditions.description: Autoimmune attack on thyroid gland; most common cause of hypothyroidism
conditions.functional_root: Gut permeability (leaky gut), gluten sensitivity (molecular mimicry), heavy metal toxicity, EBV reactivation, high stress
conditions.conventional_approach: Treat resulting hypothyroidism with levothyroxine; autoimmune aspect largely ignored conventionally

conditions.name: PCOS
conditions.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome: androgen excess, menstrual irregularity, metabolic dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Insulin resistance drives most PCOS cases; addresses with metabolic interventions
conditions.conventional_approach: OCP for symptom management, metformin for insulin resistance, clomiphene for fertility

conditions.name: Testosterone Deficiency (Male)
conditions.description: Low testosterone with symptomatic effect: fatigue, low libido, poor recovery, depression, cognitive decline
conditions.functional_root: Chronic stress (cortisol suppresses testosterone), sleep deprivation, obesity, alcohol, cannabis, nutritional deficiency (zinc, D3)
conditions.conventional_approach: TRT (testosterone replacement therapy); often under-investigated in younger men

conditions.name: Adrenal Dysregulation
conditions.description: HPA axis dysfunction: abnormal cortisol curve, impaired stress response. Note: 'Adrenal Fatigue' is a functional label, not a conventional diagnosis
conditions.functional_root: Chronic psychological stress, sleep deprivation, blood sugar dysregulation, nutritional depletion (B5, C, magnesium)
conditions.conventional_approach: Conventional medicine does not recognise adrenal fatigue; investigates only Addison's (true adrenal insufficiency) or Cushing's

conditions.name: Major Depression
conditions.description: Persistent low mood, anhedonia, cognitive impairment, sleep and appetite disruption
conditions.functional_root: Neuroinflammation, gut dysbiosis (serotonin production), nutritional deficiency (omega-3, zinc, B vitamins, D3), HPA dysregulation, unresolved trauma
conditions.conventional_approach: SSRIs/SNRIs first line; therapy (CBT, ACT); lifestyle evidence strong but under-utilised

conditions.name: Generalised Anxiety Disorder
conditions.description: Excessive worry, hypervigilance, somatic symptoms, autonomic arousal
conditions.functional_root: HPA dysregulation, sympathetic dominance, gut-brain axis (anxiogenic microbiome), magnesium deficiency, caffeine excess, thyroid dysregulation
conditions.conventional_approach: SSRIs/SNRIs, therapy (CBT); benzodiazepines short-term only

conditions.name: ADHD
conditions.description: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder: inattention, impulsivity, executive dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Dopamine/norepinephrine dysregulation; often exacerbated by sleep deprivation, nutritional deficiency (omega-3, zinc, iron), gut dysbiosis, screen overexposure
conditions.conventional_approach: Stimulant medication (methylphenidate, amphetamines) first line; therapy adjunctive

conditions.name: Burnout
conditions.description: Chronic workplace/life stress leading to exhaustion, cynicism, and reduced efficacy
conditions.functional_root: Sustained HPA activation → cortisol depletion → loss of stress buffer; not distinguishable from adrenal dysregulation biologically
conditions.conventional_approach: Rest, therapy, lifestyle change; not a formal psychiatric diagnosis (ICD-11 classifies as occupational phenomenon)

conditions.name: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder
conditions.description: Intrusive memories, hypervigilance, avoidance, emotional dysregulation following trauma
conditions.functional_root: Amygdala hyperactivation, prefrontal hypoactivation, HPA axis dysregulation, somatic storage of traumatic memory
conditions.conventional_approach: Trauma-focused CBT, EMDR, medications (SSRIs/SNRIs)

conditions.name: Functional Neurological Disorder
conditions.description: Neurological symptoms (weakness, tremor, seizures, sensory) without structural lesion; brain network dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Trauma (adverse childhood experiences), predictive coding errors in brain, central sensitization, threat-mode nervous system
conditions.conventional_approach: Multidisciplinary rehabilitation: physio, OT, psychology, neurology; Pain Reprocessing Therapy, Somatic Experiencing

conditions.name: POTS (Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome)
conditions.description: Heart rate increase >30 bpm on standing; autonomic dysregulation
conditions.functional_root: Nervous system dysregulation, deconditioning, hypovolaemia, autoimmune mechanisms, mast cell activation
conditions.conventional_approach: Exercise (Levine/Dallas Protocol), increased salt/fluid, beta-blockers, fludrocortisone; specialist management

conditions.name: Dysautonomia
conditions.description: Umbrella term for autonomic nervous system dysfunction: heart rate, BP, digestion, temperature regulation
conditions.functional_root: Substance use damage, post-viral, trauma, EDS, autoimmune
conditions.conventional_approach: Depends on specific type; lifestyle modification + specialist

conditions.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
conditions.description: Functional gut disorder: altered bowel habits, bloating, abdominal pain, without structural pathology
conditions.functional_root: Gut dysbiosis, visceral hypersensitivity, gut-brain axis dysregulation, food intolerances, stress
conditions.conventional_approach: Low-FODMAP diet, antispasmodics, TCAs, SSRIs for visceral hypersensitivity; lifestyle

conditions.name: SIBO (Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth)
conditions.description: Bacteria overgrowth in small intestine causing bloating, malabsorption, IBS-type symptoms
conditions.functional_root: Impaired migrating motor complex (MMC), low stomach acid, prior antibiotic use, structural issues
conditions.conventional_approach: Rifaximin antibiotic course; elemental diet; often relapse without addressing root cause

conditions.name: Leaky Gut (Intestinal Permeability)
conditions.description: Increased intestinal permeability allowing LPS and undigested food particles into bloodstream; drives systemic inflammation
conditions.functional_root: Gluten, alcohol, NSAIDs, stress, dysbiosis, zinc deficiency
conditions.conventional_approach: Not a recognised conventional diagnosis; addressed in functional medicine

conditions.name: Crohn's Disease
conditions.description: Chronic transmural inflammation, can affect any part of GI tract
conditions.functional_root: Genetic susceptibility + gut dysbiosis + immune dysregulation + environmental triggers
conditions.conventional_approach: Biologics, steroids, immunosuppressants, surgery in severe cases; requires specialist

conditions.name: Ulcerative Colitis
conditions.description: Chronic mucosal inflammation of colon and rectum
conditions.functional_root: Similar to Crohn's; distinct pattern of inflammation
conditions.conventional_approach: Aminosalicylates, corticosteroids, biologics; requires specialist

conditions.name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS)
conditions.description: Profound fatigue, post-exertional malaise, cognitive dysfunction, sleep disturbance, orthostatic intolerance
conditions.functional_root: Post-viral immune activation, mitochondrial dysfunction, dysautonomia, HPA dysregulation, gut dysbiosis
conditions.conventional_approach: No proven treatment; PACE trial (CBT/graded exercise) was controversial; now pacing is primary approach

conditions.name: Long COVID
conditions.description: Post-COVID symptoms persisting >12 weeks: fatigue, brain fog, breathlessness, PEM, autonomic dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Viral persistence, microbiome disruption, autoimmune mechanisms, mitochondrial dysfunction, reactivated latent viruses (EBV)
conditions.conventional_approach: Emerging; supportive; specialist Long COVID clinics in NZ

conditions.name: Post-Concussion Syndrome
conditions.description: Persistent symptoms after concussion: headaches, cognitive difficulty, mood changes, fatigue, sleep disruption
conditions.functional_root: Neuroinflammation, neurovascular uncoupling, mitochondrial dysfunction, HPA dysregulation
conditions.conventional_approach: Rest, graduated return to activity; specialist (neurologist/concussion clinic); no proven pharmacological treatment

conditions.name: Chronic Back Pain
conditions.description: Persistent low back pain >12 weeks; most commonly non-specific or discogenic
conditions.functional_root: Deconditioning, central sensitization, fear-avoidance behaviour, HPA dysregulation worsening pain perception, structural factors (disc, facet)
conditions.conventional_approach: Active management (exercise) over passive; CBT for psychosocial factors; minimise opioids

conditions.name: Fibromyalgia
conditions.description: Widespread pain, fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, sleep disturbance; central sensitization syndrome
conditions.functional_root: Central sensitization, HPA dysregulation, sleep disruption, trauma history, autonomic dysfunction
conditions.conventional_approach: Low-dose amitriptyline, duloxetine, pregabalin, graded exercise, CBT; often under-managed

conditions.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
conditions.description: Autoimmune inflammatory arthritis targeting synovial joints; systemic inflammation
conditions.functional_root: Genetic susceptibility + gut dysbiosis + environmental trigger (infection, smoking); leaky gut as potential initiator
conditions.conventional_approach: DMARDs (methotrexate), biologics (TNF inhibitors); requires rheumatologist

conditions.name: Lupus (SLE)
conditions.description: Systemic autoimmune disease: multi-organ inflammation, photosensitivity, butterfly rash, renal involvement
conditions.functional_root: Genetic susceptibility, UV exposure, EBV, hormonal factors; requires specialist management
conditions.conventional_approach: Hydroxychloroquine, corticosteroids, immunosuppressants; requires rheumatologist

conditions.name: Psoriasis
conditions.description: Autoimmune skin condition with systemic inflammation; associated with metabolic syndrome and psoriatic arthritis
conditions.functional_root: Gut dysbiosis, leaky gut, alcohol, stress, nutritional deficiency, immune dysregulation
conditions.conventional_approach: Topical treatments, phototherapy, biologics for severe; dermatologist for moderate-severe

conditions.name: Eczema / Atopic Dermatitis
conditions.description: Inflammatory skin condition; skin barrier dysfunction + immune dysregulation
conditions.functional_root: Gut dysbiosis, food allergens, skin microbiome disruption, nutritional deficiency
conditions.conventional_approach: Emollients, topical corticosteroids, dupilumab for severe; dermatologist for moderate-severe

conditions.name: Asthma
conditions.description: Chronic airway inflammation and reversible airflow obstruction
conditions.functional_root: Allergic sensitization, gut-lung axis, nutritional deficiency (D3, magnesium)
conditions.conventional_approach: Inhaled corticosteroids + beta-agonists; avoid triggers; specialist for severe

conditions.name: Sleep Apnea (Obstructive)
conditions.description: Repeated upper airway collapse during sleep; intermittent hypoxia
conditions.functional_root: Obesity, anatomical factors, alcohol, muscle relaxants; significantly worsens all chronic conditions
conditions.conventional_approach: CPAP (gold standard), weight loss, positional therapy; diagnosis requires sleep study

conditions.name: Insomnia
conditions.description: Difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep; daytime impairment
conditions.functional_root: Hyperarousal (cognitive, somatic, cortical), poor sleep hygiene, caffeine, screen light, anxiety, pain, substance use
conditions.conventional_approach: CBT-I (most effective, gold standard); sleep hygiene; short-term medication if necessary

conditions.name: Chronic Inflammation (Systemic)
conditions.description: Persistent low-grade inflammation across multiple systems; underlies most chronic disease
conditions.functional_root: Visceral fat, gut dysbiosis, sleep deprivation, psychological stress, excess omega-6/omega-3 ratio, sedentary lifestyle
conditions.conventional_approach: Address underlying causes; no single treatment

conditions.name: Vitamin D Deficiency
conditions.description: Serum 25(OH)D <50 nmol/L; functional suboptimal <100 nmol/L
conditions.functional_root: Inadequate sun exposure, indoor lifestyle, melanin (darker skin needs more UV), winter in NZ
conditions.conventional_approach: Supplementation; standard NZ recommendation 400-1000 IU insufficient — functional optimal requires 2000-4000 IU

conditions.name: Magnesium Deficiency
conditions.description: Serum magnesium low or low-normal; tissue depletion more sensitive marker
conditions.functional_root: Stress depletes magnesium rapidly; soil depletion; alcohol; PPI use; high sugar/refined carb diet
conditions.conventional_approach: Supplementation; form matters enormously — glycinate > citrate >> oxide

conditions.name: Iron Deficiency Anaemia
conditions.description: Insufficient iron for haemoglobin synthesis; fatigue, cognitive impairment, breathlessness
conditions.functional_root: Inadequate dietary intake, poor absorption (low stomach acid, gluten sensitivity), heavy menstruation, gastrointestinal blood loss
conditions.conventional_approach: Oral iron supplementation; investigate cause; IV iron if severe/non-responsive

conditions.name: B12 Deficiency
conditions.description: Insufficient vitamin B12; neurological and haematological consequences
conditions.functional_root: Vegan/vegetarian diet, PPI/metformin use, intrinsic factor deficiency (pernicious anaemia), low stomach acid
conditions.conventional_approach: Supplementation (methylcobalamin preferred over cyanocobalamin for MTHFR variants); IM injection for severe/absorption issues

conditions.name: Omega-3 Deficiency
conditions.description: Insufficient EPA/DHA; pro-inflammatory state, cognitive impairment, mood dysregulation
conditions.functional_root: Low oily fish intake; high omega-6 diet (seed oils); NZ/Western diet
conditions.conventional_approach: Supplementation with EPA/DHA-rich fish oil; food sources (salmon, mackerel, sardines)

conditions.name: Zinc Deficiency
conditions.description: Insufficient zinc; immune dysfunction, wound healing impairment, testosterone reduction, cognitive effects
conditions.functional_root: Alcohol consumption, stress, plant-based diet (phytates reduce absorption), PPI use
conditions.conventional_approach: Supplementation; pair with copper (1-2mg) at doses >25mg zinc to prevent copper displacement

conditions.name: Coeliac Disease
conditions.description: Autoimmune reaction to gluten causing small intestinal damage and malabsorption
conditions.functional_root: Genetic susceptibility (HLA-DQ2/DQ8) + gluten exposure; requires strict gluten avoidance
conditions.conventional_approach: Strict lifelong gluten-free diet; gastroenterologist; monitor for nutritional deficiencies

conditions.name: Non-Coeliac Gluten Sensitivity
conditions.description: Symptoms triggered by gluten without coeliac or wheat allergy; mechanism contested
conditions.functional_root: Possible ATI (amylase-trypsin inhibitor) reaction, FODMAP component (fructans), immune activation
conditions.conventional_approach: Elimination diet; not currently recognisable by conventional blood tests

conditions.name: Osteoporosis
conditions.description: Reduced bone mineral density; increased fracture risk
conditions.functional_root: Calcium/D3/K2/magnesium deficiency, low exercise (especially resistance training), low oestrogen/testosterone, smoking, alcohol, corticosteroid use
conditions.conventional_approach: Calcium, D3, bisphosphonates for high fracture risk; bone density scan for diagnosis

conditions.name: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome (hEDS)
conditions.description: Hereditary connective tissue disorder; hypermobility, joint instability, chronic pain, fatigue, autonomic dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Genetic; no cure; management of symptoms and complications
conditions.conventional_approach: Physiotherapy, pain management, POTS management if comorbid; geneticist/rheumatologist for diagnosis

conditions.name: Mould Illness (CIRS)
conditions.description: Chronic inflammatory response syndrome from water-damaged building biotoxins; multi-system illness
conditions.functional_root: Genetic susceptibility (HLA-DR); biotoxin retention in those who cannot clear; triggers systemic inflammation
conditions.conventional_approach: Remove exposure; specific treatment protocol (cholestyramine, VIP, etc.); specialist functional medicine

conditions.name: Heavy Metal Toxicity
conditions.description: Accumulated heavy metals (lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium) causing neurological, immune, and metabolic dysfunction
conditions.functional_root: Dental amalgam (mercury), contaminated water/food, occupational exposure; genetic susceptibility (impaired detoxification)
conditions.conventional_approach: Chelation therapy if significant levels confirmed; remove source; specialist required

conditions.name: Kidney Stones
conditions.description: Crystalline deposits in renal system; can cause severe pain, obstruction
conditions.functional_root: Dehydration, high oxalate diet (in calcium oxalate type), low citrate, hyperuricaemia, anatomical factors
conditions.conventional_approach: Hydration, dietary modification; lithotripsy or surgical removal if large; urology if recurrent

conditions.name: Hypertension
conditions.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure; major cardiovascular risk factor
conditions.functional_root: Insulin resistance, sleep apnea, stress/HPA activation, high sodium/low potassium diet, alcohol, sedentary lifestyle, obesity
conditions.conventional_approach: Lifestyle first; ACE inhibitors, ARBs, calcium channel blockers, diuretics; GP management

conditions.name: Dysbiosis (Gut Microbiome Imbalance)
conditions.description: Imbalanced gut microbiome: reduced diversity, pathobiont overgrowth, beneficial bacteria depletion
conditions.functional_root: Antibiotics, high sugar/processed food diet, stress, alcohol, low fibre, sedentary lifestyle
conditions.conventional_approach: Not a conventional diagnosis; addressed through diet, probiotics, prebiotics

conditions.name: Endometriosis
conditions.description: Endometrial-like tissue outside uterus; pelvic pain, heavy periods, infertility, fatigue
conditions.functional_root: Immune dysregulation, oestrogen dominance, retrograde menstruation theory; average 7-10 year diagnostic delay
conditions.conventional_approach: Hormonal management (OCP, GnRH analogues), surgery (laparoscopy); requires gynaecologist/specialist

conditions.name: Central Sensitization
conditions.description: Amplified pain signalling in CNS; normal stimuli perceived as painful; underlies many chronic pain conditions
conditions.functional_root: Trauma, sustained stress, nervous system dysregulation, sleep deprivation; learned threat response in nervous system
conditions.conventional_approach: Pain neuroscience education, graded exercise, CBT, low-dose TCA; Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT)

interventions.name: Zone 2 Cardio
interventions.mechanism: Increases mitochondrial density, BDNF, cerebral blood flow, metabolic flexibility, parasympathetic tone
interventions.dose_notes: 45-60 min at conversational pace (nasal breathing), 3-5x/week. Heart rate: 60-70% max.

interventions.name: Resistance Training
interventions.mechanism: Insulin sensitivity, testosterone, BDNF, bone density, body composition, longevity
interventions.dose_notes: 2-4x/week, progressive overload, compound movements

interventions.name: Sleep Optimisation
interventions.mechanism: Glymphatic clearance, memory consolidation, hormonal regulation, immune function, cortisol rhythm
interventions.dose_notes: 7.5-9 hours, consistent window, dark/cool room, no screens 45+ min pre-bed

interventions.name: Magnesium Glycinate
interventions.mechanism: NMDA receptor regulation, GABA activation, cortisol reduction, ATP production, muscle relaxation
interventions.dose_notes: 200-400mg elemental magnesium before bed. Glycinate form for best absorption and minimal GI effects.

interventions.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil (EPA/DHA)
interventions.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, neuronal membrane fluidity, serotonin and dopamine receptor sensitivity, triglyceride reduction
interventions.dose_notes: 2-4g EPA+DHA daily. EPA:DHA ratio ~3:2 for mood/brain. With food (fat increases absorption).

interventions.name: Vitamin D3 + K2
interventions.mechanism: Immune modulation, calcium metabolism, testosterone production, mood regulation, anti-inflammatory
interventions.dose_notes: 2000-4000 IU D3 daily, paired with K2 MK-7 (100-200mcg) to direct calcium to bones. Test levels — target 100-150 nmol/L.

interventions.name: Creatine Monohydrate
interventions.mechanism: ATP regeneration, neuroprotection, cognitive function, strength, muscle mass
interventions.dose_notes: 3-5g/day. No loading phase needed. With food. Well-tolerated — among most studied supplements.

interventions.name: Zinc Bisglycinate
interventions.mechanism: Immune function, testosterone synthesis, wound healing, taste/smell, antioxidant
interventions.dose_notes: 15-30mg/day with food. Always pair >25mg with 1-2mg copper to prevent depletion.

interventions.name: B12 (Methylcobalamin)
interventions.mechanism: Neurological function, myelin synthesis, red blood cell production, homocysteine reduction
interventions.dose_notes: 500-2000mcg/day sublingually for best absorption. Methylcobalamin preferred over cyanocobalamin (especially for MTHFR).

interventions.name: Ashwagandha KSM-66
interventions.mechanism: Cortisol reduction, HPA axis modulation, testosterone support, anxiety reduction, thyroid T4 support
interventions.dose_notes: 300-600mg KSM-66 extract daily. Can split morning/evening. 8-12 week cycle.

interventions.name: Rhodiola Rosea
interventions.mechanism: Adaptogenic: cortisol modulation, fatigue resistance, cognitive performance under stress, mild antidepressant
interventions.dose_notes: 200-400mg standardised extract (3% rosavins, 1% salidroside) morning. Stimulating — avoid evening.

interventions.name: L-Theanine
interventions.mechanism: Alpha wave promotion, GABA enhancement, anxiety reduction without sedation, synergistic with caffeine
interventions.dose_notes: 100-200mg. With caffeine (100mg theanine : 50mg caffeine ratio) for clean focus. Or alone for anxiety.

interventions.name: Lion's Mane Mushroom
interventions.mechanism: NGF (nerve growth factor) stimulation, neurogenesis, ADHD symptom reduction, neuroprotection
interventions.dose_notes: 500-1000mg/day of fruiting body extract. Effects build over weeks.

interventions.name: Low-FODMAP Diet
interventions.mechanism: Reduces fermentable carbohydrates driving gut dysbiosis symptoms; IBS symptom reduction
interventions.dose_notes: Strict elimination 4-6 weeks then structured reintroduction. Use with dietitian ideally.

interventions.name: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
interventions.mechanism: Reduces systemic inflammation via omega-3 increase, polyphenols, elimination of refined carbohydrates and seed oils
interventions.dose_notes: Mediterranean-style: oily fish 3x/week, colourful vegetables, olive oil, minimal refined carbs and seed oils

interventions.name: Cold Exposure
interventions.mechanism: Norepinephrine spike, BDNF, stress resilience, brown fat activation, mood elevation
interventions.dose_notes: 30-60 second cold shower end daily. Or 2-3 min cold immersion 3x/week. Not immediately before sleep.

interventions.name: Breath Work (Physiological Sigh)
interventions.mechanism: Fastest acute stress reduction intervention with RCT support; deflates air sacs, clears CO2
interventions.dose_notes: 2x inhale through nose (first fills lungs, second tops them up), then long slow exhale. 1-3 repetitions.

interventions.name: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT)
interventions.mechanism: Restructures maladaptive thought patterns; evidence base across anxiety, depression, insomnia, chronic pain
interventions.dose_notes: 8-16 sessions typically. CBT-I for insomnia is gold standard.

interventions.name: Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT)
interventions.mechanism: Breaks fear-pain cycle through safety reappraisal; 66% pain-free in RCT (JAMA Psychiatry 2025)
interventions.dose_notes: 8-session protocol; trained PRT therapist. Applicable to chronic pain and FND.

interventions.name: Rifaximin
interventions.mechanism: Non-absorbable antibiotic targeting small intestinal bacteria; SIBO treatment
interventions.dose_notes: 550mg 3x/day for 14 days. Prescription required. Relapse common without addressing root cause.

interventions.name: Levothyroxine (T4)
interventions.mechanism: Synthetic T4 thyroid hormone replacement
interventions.dose_notes: Morning, fasting, 30-60 min before food. Separate from calcium, iron, biotin by 4 hours.

interventions.name: SSRIs
interventions.mechanism: Serotonin reuptake inhibition; antidepressant, anxiolytic, visceral pain modulation
interventions.dose_notes: First-line for depression, anxiety, IBS visceral hypersensitivity. Requires GP prescription.

interventions.name: Methylfolate (5-MTHF)
interventions.mechanism: Active folate for methylation, neurotransmitter synthesis, homocysteine reduction; bypasses MTHFR conversion
interventions.dose_notes: 400-1000mcg/day. Critical for those with MTHFR mutations — folic acid supplementation may be ineffective.

interventions.name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
interventions.mechanism: Mitochondrial electron transport chain; antioxidant; critical for those on statins (statin-induced depletion)
interventions.dose_notes: 100-300mg ubiquinol form (more bioavailable than ubiquinone). With fat. Essential supplement for statin users.

interventions.name: Probiotic (Spore-based)
interventions.mechanism: Microbiome restoration, gut barrier support, immune modulation
interventions.dose_notes: Spore-based (Bacillus coagulans/subtilis) more resilient than standard lactobacillus. 30-day minimum.

interventions.name: St John's Wort
interventions.mechanism: Antidepressant via multiple neurotransmitter pathways; comparable to SSRIs in mild-moderate depression RCTs
interventions.dose_notes: 300mg standardised extract (0.3% hypericin) 3x/day. 4-6 week onset. CRITICAL: DO NOT combine with SSRIs, warfarin, oral contraceptives, many other drugs.

red_flags.reason: Could indicate colorectal cancer, IBD, or significant GI bleed
Could indicate renal stones, UTI, bladder or kidney pathology
Haemoptysis — possible pulmonary embolism, TB, lung pathology
>5kg without intentional cause — possible malignancy, endocrine, IBD
New unexplained lump — rule out malignancy
Fever >3 days without clear cause — possible serious infection
Yellow skin/eyes — liver, bile duct, or pancreatic pathology
Sudden vision loss/change — possible retinal detachment or stroke
Intermenstrual or post-menopausal bleeding — rule out endometrial pathology
With other systemic symptoms — rule out lymphoma, TB, other

specialists.when_to_refer: Persistent neurological symptoms, suspected MS, TBI sequelae, epilepsy, unexplained movement disorder
specialists.nz_resource: GP referral; neurology wait times 6-18 months public, 2-4 weeks private

specialists.when_to_refer: Complex thyroid (not responding to T4), suspected Cushing's/Addison's, complex diabetes, pituitary issues
Autoimmune arthritis (RA, lupus, AS, psoriatic), complex connective tissue disease, EDS
IBD diagnosis/management, colonoscopy, complex GERD, persistent GI bleeding
Arrhythmia, structural heart disease, POTS (cardiology subspecialty), post-cardiac event
specialists.nz_resource: GP referral; POTS via cardiology or autonomic neurologist

specialists.when_to_refer: Treatment-resistant depression/anxiety, bipolar management, medication adjustment, severe PTSD
specialists.nz_resource: GP referral to mental health service; private psychiatrist for faster access

specialists.when_to_refer: Complex multi-system chronic illness, treatment-resistant symptoms despite conventional workup, suspected SIBO/CIRS/heavy metal toxicity
specialists.nz_resource: Private; $300-600/session; no GP referral needed. Ask for AIMA-registered practitioners.

specialists.when_to_refer: Musculoskeletal pain, post-injury rehab, POTS exercise protocol, chronic back pain, pelvic floor
specialists.nz_resource: Direct access; ACC covers injury-related treatment; $80-140/session private

condition_interventions.notes: Most potent single intervention for insulin sensitivity
Muscle as glucose sink; increases GLUT4
Single night of poor sleep impairs insulin sensitivity significantly
Reduce refined carbs, seed oils; add polyphenols
Magnesium cofactor for insulin signalling
Comparable to antidepressants in mild-moderate depression (multiple RCTs)
EPA specifically — meta-analysis supports antidepressant effect
Bidirectional: depression worsens sleep, poor sleep worsens depression
D3 deficiency associated with depression; supplementation shows benefit
Evidence-based first-line pharmacological treatment
Comparable to medication; combination more effective than either alone
Significant anxiolytic effect through HPA regulation
NMDA/GABA modulation; anxiolytic effect
Alpha wave promotion, GABA; well-tolerated anxiolytic
Cortisol and anxiety reduction in RCTs
Fastest acute intervention; RCT evidence
Primary treatment when TSH elevated and symptomatic
Required for T4→T3 conversion; 100-200mcg selenomethionine
Commonly deficient in autoimmune thyroid; supports immune modulation
Strongest dietary evidence for IBS symptom reduction
Gut-directed hypnotherapy and CBT have strong IBS evidence
For constipation-predominant IBS; improves motility
Meta-analysis supports modest but consistent ADHD symptom reduction
Zinc deficiency common in ADHD; cofactor for dopamine synthesis
NGF stimulation; ADHD symptom reduction in small trials
BDNF and dopamine/norepinephrine; immediate and sustained cognitive benefit
Sleep hygiene is prerequisite
CBT-I is gold standard — more effective than medication long-term
GABA/NMDA modulation; improves sleep quality
Graduated return to exercise; increases cerebral blood flow and BDNF
Neuroprotective; neuronal membrane repair; anti-inflammatory
Neuroprotection and energy buffer; reduces severity and duration
Aerobic exercise reduces chronic pain via endorphin and anti-inflammatory mechanisms
66% pain-free at follow-up — best evidence for chronic non-structural back pain
Spinal stabilisation, pain reduction
Primary treatment; test and treat to optimal 100-150 nmol/L
Primary treatment; glycinate form best absorbed
Ferrous bisglycinate: better absorbed, less GI distress than ferrous sulphate
Primary treatment; sublingual or injection for absorption issues
Primary treatment
Dietary pattern is the most modifiable driver of chronic inflammation
Exercise reduces CRP and inflammatory cytokines
EPA/DHA shift eicosanoid balance anti-inflammatory
Sleep deprivation dramatically increases inflammatory markers

interactions.mechanism: Serotonin syndrome risk; CYP3A4 induction reduces SSRI levels then rebound on cessation
Antiplatelet effect increases bleeding risk at doses >3g/day
interactions.action: Reduce dose to <1g EPA+DHA or discuss with GP

interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces OCP plasma levels — contraceptive failure risk
Magnesium binds levothyroxine in gut, reduces absorption
Ashwagandha may increase T4/T3 levels — hyperthyroid symptoms in those on replacement
interactions.action: Monitor thyroid levels; discuss with GP

interactions.mechanism: Zinc chelates thyroid hormone in gut, reduces absorption
Potential additive serotonergic effect at high doses; theoretical
interactions.action: Standard doses (3-5g) generally considered safe; monitor

interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces warfarin — decreased anticoagulation, clot risk
Vitamin K2 is antagonist to warfarin mechanism
interactions.action: Discuss with GP before starting K2 supplementation if on warfarin
